guest: Howie Liu, co-founder and CEO of Airtable (~$10B valuation)

business_ideas.idea: Live shopping in the West powered by AI avatar hosts, automating the influencer-seller
AI-personalized news product with an interactive agent and injected personality
Real-time AI video avatars for sales and customer support interactions
AI personal-finance advisor avatar grounded in the user's real wealth data
AI-native social app where bots are co-participants, not just tools
Cursor-style AI-native reinvention of other professional tools beyond coding
AI replacing labor-heavy service businesses like BPOs and call centers

predictions.text: AI avatars become a primary interface for high-touch consumer experiences within a couple of years
The next breakout consumer social product will be AI-native rather than a retrofit
The artist phase of AI is coming — product taste will beat model proximity

disagreements: Sam pushes on whether AI-generated hosts and content lose the human authenticity that drives commerce and media; Howie argues personality can be engineered in and consumers adapt faster than expected.
dynamics: Sam runs a tight premise — 'if you were 21 again, what would you build' — and interrogates each of Howie's seven ideas for market size and defensibility. Howie is expansive and thesis-driven; Sam translates to practical terms.
frameworks: Small slice of huge TAM vs big slice of small TAM: both routes reach billion-dollar scale
Technologist phase vs artist phase: early platform waves reward model-whisperers, later waves reward product taste
New-paintbrush principle: new mediums produce unpredictable consumer behavior (TV to YouTube to TikTok)
learnings: Cursor's rise shows every incumbent workflow assumption is renegotiable once AI is native to the tool
Injecting deliberate personality into AI output is a differentiator, not a gimmick
The most exposed businesses are the most labor-intensive ones — that's where AI margin expansion is largest

banger_quotes.quote: AI doesn't have to be robotic
What is consumer psychology and behavior look like when you have this new paintbrush

numbers_dropped: Live shopping in Asia cited as a $100B+ GMV market
Cursor-style math: single-digit share of ~30M developers at ~$1,000/year is a $1B+ business
notable: Howie lays out seven concrete AI businesses he'd start today if he didn't have Airtable, all anchored in avatar and agent interfaces.
